---
title: "Privacy Policy"
subtitle: "How we handle your data"
date: "2026-07-05"
---

## 1. Controller

The controller responsible for data processing on this website is:

Dr. Nils Roemer\
Billhorner Deich 88\
20539 Hamburg\
Germany

Email: [nils.roemer@planningio.com](mailto:nils.roemer@planningio.com)

This website is a purely informational course website. It does not use web analytics, tracking, or embedded chat assistants. To exercise your rights or for any questions about data processing, please contact us using the details above.

## 2. Hosting

This website is hosted by STRATO AG, Otto-Ostrowski-Straße 7, 10249 Berlin, Germany, on servers located in Germany. When you visit this website, the hosting provider technically requires certain data to deliver the pages to your device. This includes your IP address, the date and time of your request, the pages accessed, and general browser and operating system information. This data is processed transiently in server logs to deliver the website and to ensure its security and stability; it is not merged with other data sources or used to identify individual visitors.

**Legal basis:** Art. 6(1)(f) GDPR (legitimate interest in providing and securing this website). A data processing agreement pursuant to Art. 28 GDPR is in place with the hosting provider.

## 3. Cookies and tracking

This website does not set cookies and does not use any tracking, analytics, or marketing tools. No information is stored on or read from your device, so no consent under § 25 TDDDG is required.

## 4. Your data protection rights

Under the EU General Data Protection Regulation (GDPR), you have the following rights regarding your personal data:

* **Right to access (Art. 15 GDPR)**
* **Right to rectification (Art. 16 GDPR)**
* **Right to erasure (Art. 17 GDPR)**
* **Right to restrict processing (Art. 18 GDPR)**
* **Right to data portability (Art. 20 GDPR)**
* **Right to object (Art. 21 GDPR)**

To exercise any of these rights, please contact us as the controller using the details in Section 1. You also have the right to lodge a complaint with a supervisory authority, in particular in the EU member state of your habitual residence, place of work, or the place of the alleged infringement.

## 5. Changes to this Privacy Policy

We may update this Privacy Policy from time to time. We will notify you of any significant changes by posting the new policy on this page and updating the date at the top. We encourage you to review this policy periodically.
